political.s: According to OpenSecrets, Dollar General reported $56,951 in PAC/individual contributions and $210,000 in federal lobbying spend in the 2024 election cycle, with no outside spending. Contributions historically skew toward Republican-leaning recipients, consistent with a Tennessee-headquartered discount retailer. The company has a formal Political Activities Policy but keeps its overall political engagement modest relative to its size.

charity.s: Dollar General's Literacy Foundation has donated more than $257 million to adult, family, and youth literacy programs since its founding in 1993. Through its Feeding America partnership (2021–2024), the company provided more than $4 million and the equivalent of over 37 million meals in in-kind food donations. The company also contributed over $1.2 million to the American Red Cross in 2024 for disaster relief.
charity.focus: Literacy & education, food insecurity, disaster relief

environment.s: Dollar General lags behind retail peers on environmental transparency and sustainability reporting, lacking robust CDP disclosure or comprehensive GHG reduction targets. The Campaign for Healthier Solutions gave Dollar General a C- on its retailer chemical safety report card and an F on its Bee-Friendly Retailer Scorecard, citing failure to expand its restricted substances list or address PFAS and most phthalates. The company has an internal Sustainability Committee and an Environmental Policy but its concrete commitments and third-party verified data remain limited compared to competitors like Walmart.

labor.s: Dollar General settled with OSHA in July 2024 for $12 million over widespread workplace safety violations including blocked emergency exits, blocked fire extinguishers, and unsafe storage, accruing over $26 million in proposed OSHA penalties since 2017. The company was designated a 'Severe Violator' by OSHA in 2023—the first retailer added to the expanded program—reflecting a pattern of repeated, willful violations across its 20,000+ stores. Employee compensation is ranked in the bottom 5% of similar-sized companies on Comparably, and workers have organized sustained campaigns demanding safer conditions and better wages.

dei.s: Dollar General rolled back its DEI initiatives in 2024–2025, removing 'investing in diverse teams' from its long-term operating priorities and terminating its Chief Diversity Officer amid a related lawsuit alleging the company sought to distance itself from DEI while concealing internal discrimination. The move prompted a nationwide boycott led by Rev. Jamal Bryant, backed by over 50 Black churches, citing betrayal of the company's core Black and low-income customer base. Prior to the rollback, the company held HRC Corporate Equality Index recognition and various diversity awards, reflecting a sharp reversal in its stated commitments.

animals.s: Dollar General does not sell firearms or have a firearms retail line, and does not manufacture consumer goods itself, but it retails a broad assortment of branded and private-label personal care, beauty, and cleaning products whose ingredient suppliers may conduct animal testing as required by law or regulation. The company's own Believe Beauty skincare private label is marketed as cruelty-free and vegan, but no blanket company-wide cruelty-free or Leaping Bunny certification covers all DG products. No formal corporate-wide animal testing ban has been publicly declared for all third-party branded products sold in stores.

guns.s: Dollar General does not sell firearms or ammunition in any of its stores. The company's employee handbook prohibits employees from carrying defensive weapons while on duty, though store-level enforcement of customer open-carry policies varies by location and state law. Dollar General has no involvement in firearms manufacturing or distribution.

privacy.s: Dollar General's mobile app and loyalty program collect extensive user data including precise GPS location, device identifiers, purchase history, and behavioral data shared with third-party marketing partners. The company states it does not sell or rent personally identifiable information, and California residents may opt out of data sharing under CCPA, but critics note the breadth of data collected and class-action arbitration clauses in its terms of service. No independent privacy grade from EFF or Mozilla has been publicly issued for Dollar General's digital properties.

execPay.s: CEO Todd Vasos received total compensation of approximately $8.16 million in fiscal 2024, while the median Dollar General worker earned roughly $18,868—producing a CEO-to-worker pay ratio of approximately 114:1 per Policy Matters Ohio's 2025 analysis of SEC proxy data. Employee compensation is consistently ranked in the bottom 5% of similarly sized companies, and historically the ratio reached as high as 935:1 in 2021 when the median worker earned under $18,000. Dollar General has made some executive compensation program changes in response to shareholder feedback, but the pay disparity remains stark.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
